Short Version (~10 words): 3 students are "grounded" by Columbia after stealing teacups from a peer.
Event: Columbia underclassmen Thomas Shreve, Isaac Abrahams, and Cornelius Bogert steal a set of teacups from another student’s room. As punishment, the trio is "grounded" to the College Hall of King’s College.

Short Version (~10 words): The First Pulitzer Prize is awarded.
Event: The First Pulitzer Prize is awarded.

Short Version (~10 words): Men dressed in Ku Klux Klan robes set fire to a cross on South Lawn.
Event: Twenty men dressed in Ku Klux Klan robes set fire to a 7-foot wooden cross on South Lawn.

Short Version (~10 words): Columbia student Corey Ford composes the Columbia fight song, "Roar, Lion, Roar"
Event: Columbia student Corey Ford composes the Columbia fight song, "Roar, Lion, Roar".

Short Version (~10 words): Columbia College tuition increases to $2,100 annually, a 1400% increase from 1900
Event: Columbia College tuition increases to $2,100 annually, a 1400% increase from the tuition in 1900

Short Version (~10 words): Thulani Davis and Ntozake Shange graduate from Barnard
Event: Thulani Davis and Ntozake Shange graduate from Barnard

Short Version (~10 words): Two freshmen steal 25 grams of uranium-238 using Pupin's tunnels
Event: Using the tunnels under Pupin Hall, two freshmen steal 25 grams of uranium-238.

Short Version (~10 words): Lauryn Hill drops out of Columbia, when The Fugees' album tops charts.
Event: Lauryn Hill drops out of Columbia, when The Fugees' album tops charts.

Short Version (~10 words): Students hunger strike, demanding an ethnic studies department.
Event: Four students go on a 15-day hunger strike, demanding that Columbia create a ethnic studies department. Three years later, Center for the Study of Ethnicity and Race at Columbia is founded.
